appraisal_cycles.label: Mar 21 – Apr 5, 2026
appraisal_cycles.assessment: Product-minded builder who moves fast and ships real products. Systems thinking is now proven — memory, triggers, wrap protocol, and command centre are live infrastructure that compounds. Strategic clarity strong: knows what to solve and what to park. Consistent gaps: auth permanently deferred (FEAT-036 is now 3+ cycles untouched — the single biggest unlock unshipped), passive receiving (improving but not closed — still accepts Claude output without challenging tradeoffs), mobile-first not yet reflexive (rule exists, practice lags), prompt batching (serial UI fixes still trigger repeat deploys). Biggest unlock this cycle: wrap automation is genuinely wired in (wrap_update.py + session_wrap.md + validation gate). That's infrastructure actually running, not just documented. The question heading into the next cycle: does the same systems discipline get applied to auth and production-grade security?

strengths.title: Ships real products
strengths.desc: 7+ projects deployed. Live URLs, real stack. Not prototypes — production-grade apps on free tier infra.

strengths.title: Wrap infrastructure shipped
strengths.desc: wrap_update.py + session_wrap.md + validation gate — actually running, not just documented.

strengths.title: Cross-stack fluency
strengths.desc: FastAPI, Next.js, Supabase, Redis, HTML/JS — no single-layer ceiling.

strengths.title: Feedback culture
strengths.desc: Corrects Claude in real time. Memory files accumulate real signal. Post-mortems are genuine.

strengths.title: Code review discipline
strengths.desc: Caught 5 AI-generated bugs in 1 Copilot file before merge. Reviews outputs, not just accepts.

strengths.title: Learning as output
strengths.desc: user_learning_log is a first-class deliverable alongside shipping. Every session adds signal.

gaps.title: Auth permanently deferred
gaps.desc: FEAT-036 — 3 cycles, 0 progress. No product has real auth. Everything is demo-access.
gaps.fix: Ship auth on one product next cycle. No more deferral.

gaps.title: Passive receiving
gaps.desc: Accepts Claude output without challenging tradeoffs. Outputs ship without question.
gaps.fix: Challenge one technical decision per session before approving.

gaps.title: Mobile-first reflex
gaps.desc: Mobile added post-build, not pre-build. Rule in place, practice lags.
gaps.fix: Design for 375px first — write mobile layout before desktop.

gaps.title: Prompt batching
gaps.desc: UI fixes arrive as serial single messages → repeat deploy cycles.
gaps.fix: Collate all issues into one list before sending. One deploy per batch.
